REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS TO DEFENDANTS AMENDED ANSWER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOTION SET FOR HEARING WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE (WHICH IS THE 3RD TRIAL DATE IN THIS ACTION). THE TITLE OF THE MOTION, THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND THE NOTICE OF THE MOTION DO NOT ALIGN: THE TITLE OF THE MOTION INDICATES THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO THE "AMENDED ANSWER," A PLEADINGS THAT THIS NOT ON FILE AND IS NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT; THE NOTICE OF MOTION PURPORTS TO SEEK STATUTORY JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO ANSWER; THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES INCLUDE ARGUMENTS PERTAINING TO BOTH, THE ANSWER AND AMENDED ANSWER. MOVING PARTY PROVIDES NO LEGAL BASIS FOR THE COURT TO CONSIDER AN UNFILED AMENDED ANSWER (ATTACHED DIRECTLY TO THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES WITHOUT ANY ATTEMPT TO PROPERLY INTRODUCE IT) ON A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT WAS ENTITLED TO AND WAS GRANTED ATTORNEY'S FEES TO "ENFORCE" THE AGREEMENT, DEFENDANT DOES NOT GET A SECOND BITE OF THE APPLE. DEFENDANT COULD HAVE AND SHOULD HAVE ASKED FOR ALL THEIR FEES TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AT THAT TIME. DEFENDANT CITES NO AUTHORITY THAT THEY CAN ASK FOR FEES NOW, FOR WORK PERFORMED BEFORE THE ORIGINAL MOTION. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $72,557 FOR FEES ON APPEAL. DEFENDANT ORIGINALLY SOUGHT $74,942 FOR FEES INCURRED STARTING ON MARCH 14, 2025 THROUGH FEBRUARY 17, 2026. HOWEVER, THE FEES INCURRED FROM MARCH 14 THROUGH MARCH 26, 2025 HAD NOTHING TO DO WITH APPELLATE WORK. THE COURT ALSO FINDS SOME OF THE FEES DUPLICATIVE (9/2/25, 9/15/25), OR NOT RELATING TO THE APPEAL (2/11/26). DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $2,277 FOR ENFORCEMENT EFFORTS RELATED TO THE JUDGMENT, STARTING ON FEBRUARY 18, 2026 THROUGH MARCH 30, 2026. THE COURT FINDS SOME OF THE WORK PERFORMED DID NOT HAVE TO DO WITH ENFORCEMENT EFFORTS BECAUSE THEY RELATE TO RESEARCH REGARDING PRIOR LAWSUITS PLAINTIFF HAS FILED OR MATTERS RELATED TO A POTENTIAL LAWSUIT FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION (2/18/26, 2/19/26, 3/12/26, 3/13/26, 3/27/26, 3/30/26). NONE OF THOSE TASKS RELATE TO ENFORCEMENT EFFORTS OF THE JUDGEMENT. WITH RESPECT TO COSTS, DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $422.70 FOR THE FILING FEES INCURRED ON APPEAL. THE COURT CANNOT DISCERN WHAT OTHER COSTS SHOULD BE AWARDED AND WHAT THEY WERE FOR. THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF FEES AND COSTS AWARDED IS $75,256.70.PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B).JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Jun-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Lindsay Hoopes, Esq. for Plaintiff Kazuko K. Artus.
Alber Flor, Esq. for Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association.

Hearing held.

Plaintiff contests the cost of appeal.
Defendant submits to the tentative ruling and argues in support of.

After hearing the arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCATION A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT MUTUAL BENEFIT CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees

is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant is not awarded any fees they ask for now for work performed in enforcing the settlement agreement. To the extent that Defendant was entitled to and was granted attorney's fees to "enforce" the agreement, Defendant does not get a second bite of the apple. Defendant could have and should have asked for all their fees to enforce the settlement agreement at that time. Defendant cites no authority that they can ask for fees now, for work performed before the original motion.
Defendant is awarded $72,557 for fees on appeal. Defendant originally sought $74,942 for fees incurred starting on March 14, 2025 through February 17, 2026. However, the fees incurred from March 14 through March 26, 2025 had nothing to do with appellate work. The Court also finds some of the fees duplicative (9/2/25, 9/15/25), or not relating to the appeal (2/11/26).
Defendant is awarded $2,277 for enforcement efforts related to the judgment, starting on February 18, 2026 through March 30, 2026. The Court finds some of the work performed did not have to do with enforcement efforts because they relate to research regarding prior lawsuits Plaintiff has filed or matters related to a potential lawsuit for malicious prosecution (2/18/26, 2/19/26, 3/12/26, 3/13/26, 3/27/26, 3/30/26). None of those tasks relate to enforcement efforts of the Judgement.
With respect to costs, Defendant is awarded $422.70 for the filing fees incurred on appeal. The Court cannot discern what other costs should be awarded and what they were for.
The total amount of fees and costs awarded is $75,256.70.

Off Calendar Oct-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jason E. Fellner and Hannibal Huntley; Fellner Law Group; 415-658-9253; appearing as counsel for Plaintiff Kazuko K. Artus.
Albert Flor Jr.; Fennemore Wendel; 510-834-6600; appearing as counsel for defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS TO DEFENDANTS AMENDED ANSWER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Motion set for hearing within 30 days of the current trial date (which is the 3rd trial date in this action). The title of the motion, the points and authorities and the Notice of the motion do not align: the title of the motion indicates that Plaintiff seeks judgment on the pleadings as to the "amended answer," a pleadings that this not on file and is not properly before the Court; the notice of motion purports to seek statutory judgment on the pleadings as to Answer; the points and authorities include arguments pertaining to both, the answer and amended answer. Moving party provides no legal basis for the Court to consider an unfiled amended answer (attached directly to the points and authorities without any attempt to properly introduce it) on a motion for judgment on the pleadings.
